---
title: "Course Catalog"
---
# Course Catalog
This catalog provides an overview of the courses offered in the Geospatial Masterclass. Each course includes a description, objectives, prerequisites, important links, and details about the instructors, duration, level, and software used.

| Title | Description | Teachers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Data Science at the World Bank](courses/data_science.qmd) | Foundations of geospatial data science | Benjamin Stewart et al. |
| [EO Basics](courses/eo_basics.qmd) | Introduction to Earth Observation (EO) and its applications in development projects | Katie McWilliams and Clara Ivanescu |
| [World Bank Boundaries (WB-GAD) and Cartography](courses/wb_gad.qmd) | Introduction to WB-GAD, legal requirements, and how to access and use the data for generating official World Bank maps | Katie McWilliams, Bruno Bonansea, Divyanshi Wadhwa, Maarten Lambrechts |
| Nighttime lights (Blackmarblepy) | How to access and map nighttime lights data through NASA’s Black Marble product | Rob Marty and Sahiti Sarva |
| [Market Access and GOSTnets](courses/health_access.qmd) | Travel time and market access are common geospatial data requests, and GOST has existing python tools to do so | Benjamin Stewart and Dunstan Matekenya |
| [Space2Stats](courses/s2s_introduction.qmd) | The World Bank’s database of sub-national geospatial aggregates. Users will explore access the data through the Python library, DDH, and the hexagon FastAPI. | Benjamin Stewart and Gabe Levin |
| [Radar damage assessments](courses/sar.qmd) | Collection of tools developed by GOST team for extracting information from SAR data | Clara |

# Forthcoming courses
| Title | Description | Teachers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Geospatial data collection | Survey solutions, Mapillary, OpenStreetMap, KoboToolbox | Bernhard Metz |
| Spatial anonymization and geo-variables | TBD | Dunstan |
| [GeoE3 (Enabling Environments for Employment)](https://worldbank.github.io/GEEST/README.html) | powerful and user-friendly open-source spatial mapping tool that enables a comprehensive analysis of how various spatial factors influence women's employment and business opportunities in any geographic area of interest | Clara |
